title: Campaign closure (0.7.2 release + final scoreboard)
cut_date_note: Dated 2026-06-01 to match the rest of the campaign and the operator's local date (HITL decision); the literal UTC clock had just rolled to 2026-06-02 at cut time.

version_bump.axis_w: 0.7.1 -> 0.7.2 (Cargo [workspace.package] + 8 workspace crates via version.workspace, python pyproject, ts package.json, Cargo.lock)

changelog.pr7_batch_collapse_honesty: Documented: degradation-only detector cannot flag the 2026-05-27 batch-collapse bug itself (it masqueraded as improved recall 1.0); it flags the regression-shaped correction (1.0 -> 0.1572 at 4a95cfd). Row-count/anomaly rule logged as future work.
changelog.pr3_reframe_honest: Latency measurement framed as tiered + local-once-per-release + CI read-path smoke, NOT canonical CI N=1M.
changelog.pr2_reframe_honest: Recall 0.937 framed as ANN-fidelity vs 0.571 IR ceiling; not retconned to IR-perfect.
changelog.070_071_reconciliation: Fixed pre-existing in-flight CHANGELOG mod: v0.7.1 is the first published 0.7.x; v0.7.0 is a local historical marker, never published. Removed all text implying v0.7.0 was published.
changelog.removed_section: (none) — no public symbols removed across the campaign; removal-detect linter PASS

dod_checklist.1_v070_v071_pushed: DONE (reframed) — v0.7.1 PUBLISHED (PR-4 c893d8b); v0.7.0 intentionally held as a local historical marker per HITL, never published. Not a literal 'both pushed' but the HITL-decided disposition.
dod_checklist.3_canonical_n1m: REFRAMED (NOT original mandate) — PR-3 found real-embedder N=1M infeasible on CI (~166h seed); replaced with local-once-per-release measurement + CI read-path smoke + HITL-locked tiered latency budget in ADR-0.7.0-text-query-latency-gates-revised.md. The substantive equivalent shipped; the box wording predates the reframe. NOT retconned to claim canonical-CI N=1M ran.
dod_checklist.6_devloop_gates: DONE — PR-6 e2c886d; ~16s warm <=30s; both regressions RED-shows verified (structural assert catches batch-collapse; catastrophic ceiling catches scanner).
dod_checklist.7_perf_history_backfill_bin_ci: DONE — PR-7 84446ef; 9 rows backfilled; jitter fixture exit 0 (no false positives); CI -> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY.

hitl.doc_archive_decision: do it now (HITL 2026-06-02) -> resolved IN PLACE (no relocation) after finding ~120 cross-referenced prompt paths.
hitl.push_approval: Approved sequence ending in tag push; executed.

push.main: 9a8e11b..34392b3 (14 commits, clean fast-forward); 0.7.1 tip c893d8b confirmed in history

dry_run_rehearsal.result: GREEN — verify-release + 5-platform builds + cargo publish dry-run t1-t7 (correct tier order embedder-t4 before engine-t5) + npm dry-run; pypi/post-publish-smoke/github-release correctly skipped under dry_run=true

publish_pipeline.first_attempt: PARTIAL — all publishes SUCCEEDED (cargo t1-t7, pypi, npm); post-publish-smoke(npm-package) FAILED in 1.6s on npm registry-propagation lag (package was published but not yet resolvable at install time); github-release SKIPPED via needs-cascade.
publish_pipeline.recovery: gh run rerun --failed (publishes idempotent, did not re-run); npm smoke passed on rerun (propagation settled; verified locally first via smoke-npm-package.sh exit 0); github-release then ran GREEN.
publish_pipeline.final_state: ALL GREEN — crates.io (embedder-api 0.6.0 held + schema/query/embedder/engine/facade/cli 0.7.2), PyPI fathomdb 0.7.2 (latest), npm fathomdb 0.7.2 (latest), GitHub release v0.7.2 (published, not draft, not prerelease, 5 python wheels attached).
publish_pipeline.honest_note: Mirrors PR-4's transparency: a post-publish SMOKE flaked on registry propagation (not a publish failure); recovered by rerunning the failed job.

doc_archive_hygiene.finding: ~120 distinct prompt paths cross-referenced by path from ~140 ADR/design/impl/run files (incl. immutable historical run logs). Physical relocation would break references or force rewriting immutable logs -> harmful, not hygiene.
doc_archive_hygiene.action: dev/plans/README.md de-staled (falsely claimed '0.6.0 only'); in-place archive convention adopted (status lives in per-campaign STATUS ledger, not filesystem location; completed-release artifacts not relocated). No files moved.

carried_items_final.ac019_idle_box_rerun: RESOLVED (PR-3) — contention artifact, not a regression.

follow_ups: GitHub flagged 1 moderate Dependabot vulnerability on the default branch (security/dependabot/2) — pre-existing, NOT introduced by PR-8, NOT a 0.7.2 blocker. Triage separately.
PR-7 future work: row-count / implausible-perfection anomaly rule to catch the batch-collapse class of bug directly (logged in dev/design/perf-regression-detection.md).
0.8.x agent-memory work (untracked dev/adr/ADR-0.8.0-agent-memory-retrieval-and-identity.md + dev/design/agent-memory-impl-strategy.md) is the next campaign — out of 0.7.2 scope, left untouched.
softprops/action-gh-release + several actions on Node 20 (deprecated June 2026) — release.yml maintenance item.
perf-canonical.yml / release.yml: actionlint skipped by local pre-push hook (not on PATH); CI enforces it.
campaign_narrative: 0.7.2 closes the RELEASE-HARDENING campaign. Phase A took the held 0.7.0+0.7.1 work to a published, validated release: doc-drift sweep (PR-1), embedder concurrency hardening (PR-9), recall-floor reframe (PR-2 family — the 'gap' was a measurement artifact; ANN-fidelity 0.937, 0.90 floor holds), tiered real-corpus latency budget (PR-3), and the 0.7.1 publish (PR-4). Phase B hardened the test/perf foundation for the 0.8.x agent-memory work: corpus-driven test harness (PR-5), always-on dev-loop perf gates (PR-6), append-only perf-regression detection (PR-7). fathomdb 0.7.2 is the post-1.0-foundation cut. CAMPAIGN CLOSED.
